title: Bishop Cross-Cover
setup: Your bishop on **c8** and king on **e6** wall off the entire 7th rank — the queen just needs the back rank.
hint: Slide the queen to the **8th rank** — the bishop on c8 already pins down {{d8}} and {{f8}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

A beautiful **queen + bishop back-rank mate**. The bishop on c8 doesn't just cover the 8th rank — it secures key flight squares while the queen delivers from the corner.

Play {{Qh8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{d7}}, {{e7}}, {{f7}} — all controlled by your king on **e6**
- {{f8}} — covered by the queen on h8 (8th rank)
- {{d8}} — covered by the bishop on **c8**
- The king cannot capture the queen: e8 to h8 is three squares away
- Nothing can interpose on the 8th rank between h8 and e8 (only the bishop on c8, beyond e8)

> **Takeaway:** A bishop on a long diagonal that touches the back rank is a quiet but lethal mating piece. When the queen lines up on the same rank, every escape vanishes.
